title: Alpha feedback gate owner override
summary: Close the alpha feedback gate by explicit user override after a fresh alpha RC pass, without pretending external child-session notes exist, and select Source to Shore beta as the next direction.

execution_order.goal: Record the packet 160 gate constraints and distinguish an owner override from real external playtest synthesis.
execution_order.why_now: The user explicitly asked to proceed to Source to Shore if the current check passed.

execution_order.goal: Run the alpha RC check and write the gate decision report.
execution_order.why_now: A stable RC is the safest available gate when real packet 159 session notes are absent.

execution_order.goal: Review the gate decision for honesty, scope control, and Source to Shore readiness.
execution_order.why_now: The project should proceed with clear evidence boundaries rather than silently erasing the original playtest requirement.

completion_gate: The report states that no real external packet 159 playtest notes were available.
The report records the user-approved override and the passing alpha RC check.
The decision selects Source to Shore beta while preserving the no biome-six/no planner/no combat/no inventory guardrails.
guardrails: Do not claim child-session evidence that does not exist.
Do not add biome six, season three, broader planner/dashboard, crafting, combat, inventory, economy, or direct API field guide.
Proceed to Source to Shore only because the user explicitly requested it and alpha:rc passed.
post_packet_path: Packet 166 may implement the first Source to Shore vertical slice directly under the owner override.
Real external playtest sessions should still use packet 159 materials later.
